_comment: data/horologion/vespers-weekday-troparion-meta.json — Horologion v2.0
PURPOSE: Documents the weekday troparion (apolytikion) dependency model
for Mon–Fri Byzantine Vespers. This file contains NO troparion text.
It records what is structurally known about the slot without fabricating
Menaion content that does not exist in this repository.
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS:
The weekday troparion at Vespers is drawn from the Menaion — the daily
saints calendar. There is no single fixed Octoechos weekday troparion
cycle equivalent to the stichera cycle. The engine can identify the
current Octoechos tone and the standard Byzantine weekday liturgical
theme for each day, but cannot produce actual troparion text without
Menaion data. This file makes that dependency contract explicit and
machine-readable, supporting better rubric output and future resolution.
WEEKDAY LITURGICAL THEMES (standard Byzantine Octoechos schema):
These themes govern which class of saints is commemorated each weekday
when no specific Menaion feast of higher rank overrides. They inform the
troparion choice — e.g., on an ordinary Monday with no ranked feast,
a troparion to the Bodiless Powers may be used. On days with a ranked
Menaion commemoration, the Menaion troparion takes precedence.
RESOLUTION DEPENDENCY CHAIN:
1. Determine if a Great Feast or ranked feast falls on this date.
If yes: use the feast's proper apolytikion from the Menaion.
2. Determine if there is a Menaion saint of rank 4+ for this date.
If yes: use the saint's apolytikion from the Menaion.
3. If no ranked Menaion commemoration: use the weekday theme troparion
from the Octoechos for the current tone and weekday theme.
(Step 3 text is NOT available in this repo without Menaion import.)
WHAT IS NOT YET IMPLEMENTED:
- Menaion saint data with troparion text
- Feast-rank override engine
- Octoechos generic weekday troparia (distinct from the stichera corpus)
These are shorter 1-stanza troparia, not the resurrectional apolytikia.
SOURCE FOR FUTURE IMPLEMENTATION:
Antiochian Service Book / OCA Menaion (12 volumes)
Jordanville Horologion (2008) weekday troparion tables

meta.note: weekday_themes below may be used by the engine to produce an accurate rubric even without troparion text

monday.theme_note: Monday commemorates the Bodiless Powers — the angelic orders, headed by the Archangels Michael and Gabriel. On ordinary Mondays, the troparion addresses the holy Angels.
monday.troparion_tone_rule: Current Octoechos weekly tone; or Menaion saint's tone if ranked commemoration present.
monday.troparion_source: Menaion (if ranked feast) or Octoechos weekday theme (if ordinary)

tuesday.theme: St. John the Baptist and the Prophets
tuesday.theme_note: Tuesday commemorates St. John the Baptist (the Forerunner) and the Old Testament Prophets. On ordinary Tuesdays, the troparion addresses the Forerunner.
tuesday.troparion_tone_rule: Current Octoechos weekly tone; or Menaion saint's tone if ranked commemoration present.
tuesday.troparion_source: Menaion (if ranked feast) or Octoechos weekday theme (if ordinary)

wednesday.theme_note: Wednesday commemorates the Holy Cross and the Theotokos. It is one of the two weekly fasting days. The troparion typically addresses the Cross or the Theotokos. The dismissal Theotokion is a Stavrotheotokion (Cross-Theotokion).
wednesday.troparion_tone_rule: Current Octoechos weekly tone; or Menaion saint's tone if ranked commemoration present.
wednesday.troparion_source: Menaion (if ranked feast) or Octoechos weekday theme (if ordinary)

thursday.theme: Holy Apostles and St. Nicholas of Myra
thursday.theme_note: Thursday commemorates the Holy Apostles and St. Nicholas, Archbishop of Myra. On ordinary Thursdays, the troparion addresses the Apostles and/or St. Nicholas.
thursday.troparion_tone_rule: Current Octoechos weekly tone; or Menaion saint's tone if ranked commemoration present.
thursday.troparion_source: Menaion (if ranked feast) or Octoechos weekday theme (if ordinary)

friday.theme_note: Friday commemorates the Passion of Christ — the Crucifixion — and the Theotokos in her grief. It is the second weekly fasting day. The troparion addresses the Cross with a penitential character. The dismissal Theotokion is a Stavrotheotokion.
friday.troparion_tone_rule: Current Octoechos weekly tone; or Menaion saint's tone if ranked commemoration present.
friday.troparion_source: Menaion (if ranked feast) or Octoechos weekday theme (if ordinary)

menaion_unavailable.description: No Menaion troparion text is available in this repo. The weekday theme and Octoechos tone are known. The rubric states the theme and tone accurately.
menaion_unavailable.honest_output: type:rubric with weekday theme, tone, and clear dependency note

menaion_available.description: Future state: Menaion troparion text resolved for the specific saint of the day. Not yet implemented.
menaion_available.honest_output: type:text with saint name, troparion text, tone
